name: Start Up Business Loans Pittsburgh
description_short: Pursuit Lending offers SBA-backed business loans and alternative financing in Pittsburgh, including 504, 7(a), microloans, and proprietary products up to $500K.
description_long:
Pursuit Lending operates as an SBA Preferred Lender and SBA Express Program-approved lender serving the Pittsburgh metropolitan area and Western Pennsylvania. The company has funded hundreds of businesses since FY2023 across diverse industries including breweries, salons, healthcare centers, and manufacturers. Their service area spans from neighborhoods like Lawrenceville and Squirrel Hill to surrounding suburbs including Cranberry Township and Monroeville.

Pursuit offers a comprehensive suite of financing products tailored to different business needs. Their SBA offerings include SBA 504 loans (up to $5.5M for owner-occupied commercial real estate), SBA 7(a) and Community Advantage loans (up to $350K for flexible business needs), and SBA Microloans (up to $50K). Beyond SBA products, they provide proprietary options: SmartLoan (up to $100K with flexible repayment), FlexLoan (up to $100K for Western PA startups with minimal first-year payments), Impact Loans (up to $500K at fixed rates), small business lines of credit (up to $100K), and the Pennsylvania SSBCI program (up to $500K at 7.75% fixed). They also offer SBA 504 refinancing options.

Pursuit distinguishes itself through its dual focus on government-backed SBA programs and proprietary products designed for businesses at different growth stages. Their specific tailoring to startups and early-stage companies through FlexLoan, combined with approval under SBA's Preferred Lenders Program, positions them as both accessible and credible. Customer testimonials highlight responsive service and streamlined processes across different loan types.

The primary limitation is lack of transparency regarding exact interest rates, credit requirements, and approval timelines on their website. While they advertise up to $500K financing, specific eligibility criteria remain unclear. The website provides general program descriptions but minimal detail on typical approval rates, processing times, or specific borrower requirements. Entrepreneurs should expect to contact the company directly for personalized rate quotes and terms.
pros: SBA Preferred Lender and SBA Express Program-approved, offering government-backed loans with lower down payments
Wide range of loan amounts from $50K (SBA Microloan) to $5.5M (SBA 504), serving businesses at all growth stages
FlexLoan product specifically designed for Western PA startups with minimal first-year payments, reducing early cash flow burden
Multiple use cases funded including working capital, equipment, debt refinance, and owner-occupied commercial real estate
Documented track record with customer testimonials from specific Pittsburgh-area businesses (Chubby Cheeks 3D/4D Ultrasound, Liberty Pole Spirits, Sprankle's Neighborhood Market)
Pennsylvania SSBCI program access at competitive 7.75% fixed rate for qualified borrowers
SmartLoan and FlexLoan products allow faster funding with flexible repayment terms compared to traditional SBA loans
cons: Website lacks specific interest rate ranges, making rate comparison difficult—only one fixed rate (7.75% SSBCI) disclosed
No published credit score requirements, debt-to-income guidelines, or approval timelines provided
SBA loans typically require personal guarantees and collateral; website does not clearly disclose these requirements upfront
Limited information on processing time and turnaround, particularly for proprietary products versus SBA programs
FlexLoan and Impact Loan products appear regionally limited to Western PA, excluding broader Pennsylvania coverage of other products
best_for: Established Pittsburgh-area businesses seeking $100K-$350K for working capital, equipment, or debt refinancing through flexible SBA 7(a) loans
Startup and early-stage Western PA entrepreneurs with minimal cash flow seeking FlexLoan products with deferred payments
Business owners purchasing owner-occupied commercial real estate and eligible for SBA 504 loans up to $5.5M
diagnosis: Pursuit is best for established Pittsburgh-area businesses and Western PA startups seeking government-backed SBA financing or flexible proprietary loans in the $50K-$500K range. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency on interest rates, credit requirements, and approval criteria—borrowers should request personalized quotes directly to evaluate actual competitiveness and feasibility before investing application time.
services: SBA 504 loans for owner-occupied commercial real estate (up to $5.5M)
SBA 7(a) and Community Advantage loans (up to $350K)
Pennsylvania Small Business Credit Initiative (SSBCI) program (up to $500K at 7.75% fixed)
SmartLoan proprietary product (up to $100K with flexible repayment)
FlexLoan for Western PA startups (up to $100K with minimal first-year payments)
SBA 504 refinance programs to reduce monthly payments
Small business line of credit (up to $100K with unlimited draws)
Impact Loans (up to $500K at fixed competitive rates)
